revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine]][[eu:Hiroshima Gokoku santutegia]][[fr:Hiroshima Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:広島護国神社]][[ru:Гококу-дзиндзя]]
{{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Hiroshima Gokoku Jinja'''|広島護国神社}} | image = Hiroshima-gokoku-zinzya.jpg | caption = Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|24|4.2|N|132|27|31.5|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }}
{{nihongo|'''Hiroshima Gokoku Jinja'''|広島護国神社}} is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Hiroshima|en|Hiroshima|qid=Q34664|lt=Hiroshima|1=Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.
==Overview== The original shrine was founded in 1869, the first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, in {{nihongo|Futabanosato|二葉の里}} Hiroshima. The shrine was established to mourn the Hiroshima-[[:en:Han (country subdivision)|Han]] victims of the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|qid=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}.
In 1934, it was dismantled and moved to where {{ill|Hiroshima Municipal Stadium (1957)|en|Hiroshima Municipal Stadium (1957)|qid=Q8012748|lt=Hiroshima Municipal Stadium|1=Hiroshima Municipal Stadium (1957)}} now stands, and in 1939 its name was changed to the Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine.
In 1945, it was destroyed by {{ill|Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki|en|Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki|qid=Q488|lt=the atomic bombing|1=Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki}},<ref>One of its three torii gates remained standing, and the buildings were destroyed. See http://www.pcf.city.hiroshima.jp/virtual/VirtualMuseum_e/exhibit_e/exh0702_e/exh070206_e.html {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070721171626/http://www.pcf.city.hiroshima.jp/virtual/VirtualMuseum_e/exhibit_e/exh0702_e/exh070206_e.html |date=2007-07-21 }}</ref> and was rebuilt within the confines of {{ill|Hiroshima Castle|en|Hiroshima Castle|qid=Q41977|lt=Hiroshima Castle|1=Hiroshima Castle}} in 1965 with the aid of donations from the citizens of Hiroshima.
The Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine is one of the most popular places for celebrating {{ill|Hatsumōde|en|Hatsumōde|qid=Q1032381|lt=Hatsumōde|1=Hatsumōde}} and [[Shichi-Go-San]] in Hiroshima.
== See also == *{{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|qid=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}} *{{ill|Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki|en|Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki|qid=Q488|lt=Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki|1=Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki}} *{{ill|Hiroshima Castle|en|Hiroshima Castle|qid=Q41977|lt=Hiroshima Castle|1=Hiroshima Castle}} *{{ill|Hiroshima Tōshō-gū|en|Hiroshima Tōshō-gū|qid=Q11484696|lt=Hiroshima Tōshō-gū|1=Hiroshima Tōshō-gū}} *{{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Japanese Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} *{{ill|Hatsumōde|en|Hatsumōde|qid=Q1032381|lt=Hatsumōde|1=Hatsumōde}} *[[Shichi-Go-San]] *{{ill|Hiroshima Toyo Carp|en|Hiroshima Toyo Carp|qid=Q247577|lt=Hiroshima Toyo Carp|1=Hiroshima Toyo Carp}} *[[Nagasaki Gokoku Shrine]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
== External links == {{Commons Category|Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine}} *[http://www.h-gokoku.or.jp/ Hiroshima Gokoku Shrine] (in Japanese)
